behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at the git-output-to-classification boundary, not just a pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: rename detection depends on real git behavior and invocation flags; canned fixtures can miss failures where git emits add+delete instead of rename. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: needs real subprocess + repo state, so not Integration. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text only concerns git diff categorization (no AI/ML step).
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert report shows the file only under `renamed` and not under `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: crash risk comes from real diff bytes/encoding and subprocess output handling across modules. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: faking diff text can hide decode/exit-code/path issues that appear only with real git binary diffs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: requires real git + filesystem artifacts, so not Integration. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected; behavior is binary diff robustness only, with no model decision.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert exit code is success and report includes binary file counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure depends on unborn-HEAD behavior and git command exit semantics. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: stubs can force success and hide real `git` errors for no-commit repos. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: real subprocess/OS behavior is required. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is `no commits yet` repo state handling, not AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real empty git repo with no commits, execute the CLI subprocess, and assert no error plus an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is end-to-end correctness across history traversal, git command output, parsing, and aggregation modules. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked history responses can align with implementation assumptions and hide mismatches against real git history semantics. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: verifies real repository history, so System. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected; behavior text is deterministic git-history counting only.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo with a known sequence of add/delete/rename/modify commits, run the CLI, and assert category counts equal independently computed expected counts from git history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at the boundary between index/working-tree states and CLI git-query strategy. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: fake repo adapters often collapse staged vs unstaged semantics and miss real index behavior. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: needs real git index + working tree + subprocess, so not Integration. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior concerns git state parity only.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent changes once staged and once unstaged, run the CLI in both states, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
